'''Sam Wilde''' (born 8 September 1995) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}} for the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He previously played for the [[North Wales Crusaders]], [[Warrington Wolves]], and had loan periods with the Widnes Vikings, [[Rochdale Hornets]] and the [[London Broncos]].<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41124909|title=Sam Wilde: Widnes Vikings sign Warrington back-rower on two-year deal from 2018|publisher=BBC Sport|date=1 September 2017|accessdate=7 December 2017}}</ref>

==Career==
===Warrington Wolves===
Wilde, a former Shevington Sharks junior, he made his début for the Warrington Wolves against the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]], [[Warrington]] on 2 July 2015.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/players/Sam-Wilde|title=Sam Wilde|author=|date=|work=loverugbyleague.com|accessdate=28 November 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://warringtonwolves.com/team|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140301074233/http://warringtonwolves.com/team|url-status=dead|archive-date=1 March 2014|title=Team|author=|date=|work=warringtonwolves.com|accessdate=28 November 2015}}</ref>

He played in the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|publisher=Guardian|date=8 October 2016|accessdate=7 November 2019}}</ref>

===Widnes Vikings===
The Widnes club announced on 31 October 2019 that Wilde had joined the club for the 2020 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=www.widnesvikings.co.uk|date=31 Oct 2019|accessdate=29 Dec 2020|title=Sam Wilde signs up for 2020|url=https://widnesvikings.co.uk/sam-wilde-signs-up-for-2020/}}</ref>

===Ottawa Aces===
On 12 August 2020 it was announced that Wilde had signed for the new franchise.<ref>{{cite web|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=12 Aug 2020|accessdate=29 Dec 2020|title=Ottawa Aces confirm signing of Sam Wilde|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ottawa-aces-confirm-signing-of-sam-wilde/}}</ref>

===Newcastle Thunder===
On 29 November 2020 it was announced that Wilde had signed for the [[Newcastle Thunder]] ahead of the 2021 season, after the deferment of Ottawa Aces joining the competition.<ref>{{cite web|website=www.rugby-league.com|date=29 Nov 2020|accessdate=29 Dec 2020|title=Newcastle Thunder sign Sam Wilde|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/57434/newcastle-thunder-sign-sam-wilde}}</ref>

===Widnes Vikings (re-join)===
On 10 August 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=10 Aug 2021|accessdate=31 Dec 2021|title=Sam Wilde to return to Widnes in 2022|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/sam-wilde-to-return-to-widnes-in-2022/}}</ref>

===North Wales Crusaders===
On 22 July 2024 it was reported that he had signed for the [[North Wales Crusaders]] in the [[RFL League 1]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Widnes Vikings|date=22 Jul 2024|accessdate=6 Jan 2025|title=SAM WILDE JOINS NORTH WALES CRUSADERS FOR 2025|url=https://widnesvikings.co.uk/sam-wilde-joins-north-wales-crusaders-from-2025/}}</ref>

===Widnes Vikings (re-join)===
On 14 November 2025 it was reported that he had re-joined [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=14 Nov 2025|accessdate=15 Nov 2025|author=Stephen Ibbetson|title=Widnes Vikings sign another from North Wales Crusaders|url=https://www.totalrl.com/widnes-vikings-sign-another-from-north-wales-crusaders/}}</ref>
